best_extraction.authors: Stauss T, El Majdoub F, Sayed D, Surges G, Rosenberg WS, Kapural L, Bundschu R, Lalkhen A, Patel N, Gliner B, Subbaroyan J, Rotte A, Edgar DR, Bettag M, Maarouf M
best_extraction.title: A multicenter real-world review of 10 kHz SCS outcomes for treatment of chronic trunk and/or limb pain
best_extraction.journal_name: Annals of Clinical and Translational Neurology
best_extraction.study_question: Is 10 kHz SCS effective for chronic trunk and/or limb pain in real-world practice?
best_extraction.population_assessed: 1660 patients with chronic trunk and/or limb pain trialed or implanted with 10 kHz SCS.
best_extraction.follow_up_duration: 3, 6, 12 months post-implantation; mean 8.9 months to last visit
best_extraction.outcome_measures: Responder rate, pain intensity, function, sleep, quality of life, medication intake
best_extraction.inclusion_criteria: Chronic trunk and/or limb pain; trialed or implanted with 10 kHz SCS, April 2014-January 2018.
best_extraction.answer_to_study_question: Over 70% had sustained pain relief, with improved function, sleep, and quality of life.

reasoning: Bibliographic fields were unanimous across all models. Study design conflicted, but two models chose retrospective_cohort_controlled, including one with higher confidence than the uncontrolled classification.

field_level_decisions.study_design: Preferred retrospective_cohort_controlled over case_series_uncontrolled based on majority and higher confidence.
field_level_decisions.follow_up_duration: Merged shared 3, 6, 12 month schedule with mean 8.9 month last visit.
field_level_decisions.outcome_measures: Used fuller consensus list including pain intensity from Anthropic.
